entries.definition: The practice of promoting the responsible use of antimicrobials in clinical and non-clinical settings.
An extraordinary situation requiring immediate attention from public health officials to protect the population.
The ability of microorganisms to evade or resist the effects of antimicrobials.
A substance derived from natural sources with demonstrated antimicrobial activity.
A set of strategies aimed at optimizing the use of antimicrobials, reducing resistance, and ensuring patient safety.
Substances used to treat bacterial infections by killing or inhibiting the growth of microorganisms.
Infections caused by viruses, not bacteria.
Bacteria that have developed resistance to multiple antibiotics, posing a significant threat to public health.
Substances or agents with activity against microorganisms, including bacteria and fungi.
Prescribing antibiotics only when necessary and using the most effective treatment options.
The incorrect or unwarranted use of antibiotics in clinical practice or agriculture.
The excessive or improper use of antibiotics in clinical practice or agriculture.
A set of strategies aimed at optimizing the use of antimicrobials, reducing resistance, and ensuring patient safety.
Continuous scientific investigation into new treatments, diagnostics, and strategies for combating antibiotic resistance.
Measures taken to reduce the risk of infection transmission in healthcare settings.
A substance derived from natural sources with demonstrated antimicrobial activity.
The ability of microorganisms to evade or resist the effects of antimicrobials.
